topic: fraction division word problem, reasoning about operations
latex:
Austin purchased $4$ pints of ice cream for a party. If each guest will be served exactly $\frac{2}{3}$ pint of ice cream, how many guests can Austin serve? Complete the sentence below.

To solve, we'll need to $\begin{pmatrix} \text{multiply } 2/3 \text{ by } 4 \\ \text{divide } 4 \text{ by } 2/3 \end{pmatrix}$, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL pints of ice cream} \end{pmatrix}$, but we don't know the $\begin{pmatrix} \text{NUMBER of groups} \\ \text{TOTAL pints of ice cream} \end{pmatrix}$.
answer_latex: To solve, we'll need to \textbf{divide 4 by 2/3}, because we know the SIZE of the groups and the \textbf{TOTAL pints of ice cream}, but we don't know the \textbf{NUMBER of groups}.
